title: Wire SandboxAdapter into KERNEL-001 ModelAdapter for model-process boxing
goal: Every model process Handshake spawns must be a child of a SandboxAdapter per spec §3.6.2 invariant ('no bare std::process::Command model spawns'). This MT wires the SandboxAdapter trait into the KERNEL-001 ModelAdapter so the dummy adapter shipped by KERNEL-001 is replaced by a sandbox-routed call path. Foundation for cluster C ModelRuntime adapters (LlamaCppRuntime / CandleRuntime) — those C MTs delegate engine boxing to the API surface this MT lands.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/sandbox_binding.rs that exposes `box_model_process(registry: &SandboxAdapterRegistry, spec: ModelProcessSpec) -> Result<ProcessHandle, ModelBoxingError>` translating ModelProcessSpec (high-level: model_id, gguf_path, engine_kind, work_profile_override) into a ProcessSpec then routing through SandboxAdapterRegistry::select; updates to existing KERNEL-001 ModelAdapter (likely in src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/model_adapter.rs — locate by Grep) to delegate to sandbox_binding. Tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/model_adapter_sandbox_binding_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: SandboxAdapter trait + registry + selection + default adapter (WSL2Podman) + bootstrap merged. KERNEL-001 ModelAdapter trait exists in main (verify path via Grep against the merged KERNEL-001 source).
implementation_notes: Pre-step: Grep for `pub trait ModelAdapter` and `impl ModelAdapter for DummyModelAdapter` in src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/** to locate the KERNEL-001 surface; record the exact module path in implementation_notes so the validator can re-discover. sandbox_binding.rs: define `pub struct ModelProcessSpec { model_id: String, gguf_path: PathBuf, engine_kind: EngineKind (enum LlamaCpp|Candle), work_profile_override: Option<AdapterId>, required_capabilities: BTreeSet<RequiredCapability>, env: BTreeMap<String,String>, gguf_root_bind: PathBuf, tokenizer_cache_bind: PathBuf }` and `pub fn box_model_process(registry: &SandboxAdapterRegistry, spec: ModelProcessSpec) -> Result<ProcessHandle, ModelBoxingError>` that (1) builds a ProcessSpec with binds=[ BindSpec{ host_path: spec.gguf_root_bind, guest_path: "/models/gguf", mode: ReadOnly }, BindSpec{ host_path: spec.tokenizer_cache_bind, guest_path: "/models/tokenizers", mode: ReadOnly } ] per spec §3.6.3, (2) calls `selection::select(registry, &process_spec, spec.work_profile_override.as_ref())` propagating SandboxSelectionFailure, (3) calls `adapter.spawn(process_spec).await`, (4) returns the ProcessHandle. ModelBoxingError thiserror enum wraps SandboxAdapterError + SandboxSelectionFailure + ModelGgufNotFound + WorkProfileInvalid. Update the existing KERNEL-001 ModelAdapter (or its dummy implementation) to call box_model_process when constructing a model process; the dummy adapter from KERNEL-001 stays for unit tests that don't exercise spawn. CRITICAL: a `#[deny(clippy::disallowed_methods)]` style lint or a repo-level grep test must catch any future addition of `std::process::Command::new` in src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/** or src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/model_adapter.rs (spec §3.6.2 'no bare std::process::Command model spawns'). Implement this as a Node-side grep test in .GOV/roles_shared/checks/no-bare-process-command-check.mjs (CO-OWNED with cluster A.4 if HBR-QUIET-003 has one already; coordinate via packet allowed_paths). Tests: (a) box_model_process with a missing gguf_path returns ModelGgufNotFound; (b) work_profile_override that doesn't satisfy required_capabilities returns the underlying SandboxSelectionFailure (not silent fallback); (c) successful spawn returns a ProcessHandle whose adapter_id matches the selected adapter; (d) grep test asserts no `Command::new` outside the sandbox/wsl2_podman/* and sandbox/windows_native_jail/* and sandbox/docker/* paths inside model_runtime/** and kernel/model_adapter.rs.
risk_if_missed: Without wiring, the KERNEL-001 ModelAdapter remains the dummy spawn path and AC-LOCAL-MODEL-BOXING ('every model process is a child of a SandboxAdapter') fails; cluster C ModelRuntime adapters cannot land their engine boxing.
validator_focus: (a) box_model_process is the sole route for model-process spawn; (b) gguf_root + tokenizer_cache binds are ReadOnly per spec §3.6.3; (c) selection failures propagate (no silent fallback); (d) no-bare-process-command-check passes against the touched modules.

minimum_controls: no-bare-process-command-check.mjs blocks future Command::new from sneaking into model_runtime/** or kernel/model_adapter.rs.
GGUF bind ReadOnly assertion is part of the integration test (not just a unit test) so the host-to-guest behavior is verified.
SandboxSelectionFailure propagates upward; box_model_process MUST NOT swallow the variant into a generic spawn error.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: ModelAdapter sandbox binding is fully implemented; box_model_process routes through SandboxAdapterRegistry::select; ReadOnly binds for gguf and tokenizer cache enforced; no-bare-process-command-check.mjs guards model_runtime/ + kernel/model_adapter.rs; test suite covers gguf-missing, work-profile override failures, ReadOnly bind assertion, and grep guard.
